title: Rhode Island bill proposes 24-hour bar hours during World Cup
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Local News Under the legislation, the extended hours would apply from June 11 through July 19 throughout the state.
Glasses are seen on the bar inside of Medici in downtown Providence owned by Alex Tomasso.

(Jessica Rinaldi/Globe Staff) By Annie Jonas April 13, 2026 | 4:24 PM 2 minutes to read Rhode Island lawmakers are considering a bill that would allow restaurants and bars to operate around the clock during the upcoming World Cup, potentially opening the door for increased business and tourism activity throughout the state.

title: Person wanted by ICE ‘nearly crushed’ agent with car in Quincy, officials say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Local News Local police also responded to the incident, which occurred on Bigelow Street in Quincy.
A person wanted by ICE allegedly “weaponized” their vehicle and targeted a federal agent in Quincy last Friday, according to officials.
The incident occurred when ICE agents attempted to make a “targeted arrest” on Bigelow Street in Quincy just after 11 a.

title: Police: Armed robbery suspects used gun, rock, and bear spray in Dorchester attack
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Crime Three people were arrested after victims were assaulted during a daytime robbery, police say.
Police reported confiscating several guns from Topliff Street after executing a search warrant.
Boston Police Department Three people were arrested after an armed robbery in Dorchester in which victims were attacked with a firearm, a rock, and bear spray, police said.
